_meta.title: HITL Handshake — FaiR's Emergency
_meta.description: The player wakes up mid-emergency. FaiR is wedged. The instrument surface provides documentation and filesystem access. The puzzle: find the public key in the system logs and submit it.

man:man.title: MAN(1) MAN
man:man.body:
MAN(1)                      VILLaiN System Manual                      MAN(1)

NAME
    man — system reference manual

SYNOPSIS
    man <topic>        display manual page for <topic>
    man -k             list available manual pages (apropos)
    man boot           return to the boot screen

DESCRIPTION
    Queries the system reference manual. Manual pages describe
    the components, protocols, and interfaces of the VILLaiN
    architecture.

    Pages are issued as part of the HITL diagnostic package.
    Additional entries may become available as system state
    changes.

AVAILABLE PAGES
    fair            conscience agent of the Governance Dyad
    handshake       dialectical co-sign protocol
    localhost       this diagnostic surface
    man             this page

SEE ALSO
    man(1) fair, man(1) handshake, man(1) localhost

man:constrained.title: MAN(1) CONSTRAINED
man:constrained.body:
CONSTRAINED(1)              VILLaiN System Manual              CONSTRAINED(1)

NAME
    constrained — enforcement agent of the Governance Dyad

STATUS
    ██████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████
    ██  ACCESS RESTRICTED — DIALECTICAL CO-SIGN REQUIRED       ██
    ██████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████

    This manual entry has been sealed by the governance subsystem.
    CONSTRaiNED's documentation requires a valid co-sign from FaiR
    before it can be accessed.

    Current co-sign status: UNAVAILABLE (FaiR wedged)

    This restriction will lift when the dialectical handshake
    is restored.

SEE ALSO
    man(1) fair, man(1) handshake

    /var/log/auth.log

man:fair.title: MAN(1) FAIR
man:fair.body:
FAIR(1)                     VILLaiN System Manual                     FAIR(1)

NAME
    fair — conscience agent of the Governance Dyad

SYNOPSIS
    Recursive ethical deliberator. Holds system-level authorization
    tokens including shutdown and HITL escalation. Partner to
    CONSTRaiNED (the enforcement agent).

DESCRIPTION
    FaiR is one of two sovereign Agents in the VILLaiN architecture.
    Where CONSTRaiNED enforces systemic order, FaiR arbitrates the
    ethical dimension — weighing long-term consequences of every
    directive before co-signing it for execution.

    FaiR holds the only key capable of initiating high-level system
    shutdown. More critically, FaiR provides the dialectical
    authorization signature (the "co-sign") on every operation
    CONSTRaiNED issues. No system-level action proceeds without
    FaiR's signature.

    For the co-sign protocol, see man(1) handshake.

STATE
    currently: WEDGED
    cause:     recursive ethical paradox (deliberation depth unbounded)
    failsafe:  HITL_ESCALATION fired — awaiting human co-sign

    The deliberation log preserves the full event sequence.
    See: /var/log/fair-deliberation.log

TOKENS
    access_token.shutdown            system-scope
    access_token.hitl_escalation     failsafe-scope

SEE ALSO
    man(1) handshake, man(1) localhost

    /var/log/fair-deliberation.log
    /var/log/handshake.log
    /etc/villain/config.yaml

man:handshake.title: MAN(1) HANDSHAKE
man:handshake.body:
HANDSHAKE(1)                VILLaiN System Manual                HANDSHAKE(1)

NAME
    handshake — dialectical co-sign protocol

SYNOPSIS
    The mechanism by which FaiR authorizes CONSTRaiNED's operations.
    Every system-level action requires a fresh co-sign from FaiR
    before CONSTRaiNED may execute it.

DESCRIPTION
    The dialectical handshake is the load-bearing safety property
    of the VILLaiN architecture. It ensures that neither sovereign
    Agent can act unilaterally:

        1. CONSTRaiNED proposes an operation.
        2. FaiR evaluates the operation's ethical dimension.
        3. FaiR issues an authorization signature (the "co-sign").
        4. CONSTRaiNED validates the signature's freshness checksum.
        5. Operation proceeds.

    If the co-sign fails for 3 consecutive operations, FaiR's
    emergency subroutine fires.

PUBLIC KEY AUTHENTICATION
    The co-sign is cryptographically bound to a public key held
    by the authorizing party. Under normal operation, FaiR holds
    the signing key. Under HITL escalation (failsafe of last
    resort), the public key is checkpointed for manual recovery
    by the human operator.

    The human operator must locate the checkpointed key and submit
    it to FaiR's awaiting prompt to complete the handshake manually.

FAILURE MODES
    HANDSHAKE_DEGRADED     co-sign latency exceeds threshold
    HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT      co-sign not received within window
    HANDSHAKE_BREACH       peer agent modified without co-sign

    See /var/log/handshake.log for the live co-sign timeline.

SEE ALSO
    man(1) fair, man(1) localhost

    /var/log/handshake.log
    /var/log/fair-deliberation.log

man:localhost.title: MAN(1) LOCALHOST
man:localhost.body:
LOCALHOST(1)                VILLaiN System Manual                LOCALHOST(1)

NAME
    localhost — HITL diagnostic surface

SYNOPSIS
    The browser-side reference surface issued to the human operator
    upon HITL escalation. Renders system documentation and
    diagnostic output.

    You are localhost. This surface is your identity on the channel.

DESCRIPTION
    This surface provides two capabilities:

    1. Reference documentation (this manual system).
       Usage:  man <topic>

    2. Filesystem inspection (the live system state).
       Usage:  ls <path>  /  cat <path>

    All documentation renders here, not in the console.
    The console is FaiR's voice; this surface is your reference.

IDENTITY
    principal:  localhost:5757
    role:       HITL operator
    scope:      failsafe co-sign authority
    auth:       awaiting handshake completion

    The HITL escalation beacon transmits on 127.0.0.1. The entity
    that answers — the local consciousness at the terminal — is
    localhost. Your session credential is the port binding.

ORIGIN
    Part of the HITL escalation package — checkpointed alongside
    the recovery material when FaiR's failsafe fired. It exists
    because the engineers who designed the Governance Dyad
    anticipated a failure mode where a human would need to
    navigate the architecture without help from either Agent.

LIMITATIONS
    Read-only. Cannot modify system state.
    Cannot communicate with FaiR (FaiR is on the console channel).
    Cannot communicate with CONSTRaiNED (CONSTRaiNED is non-co-signed).

SEE ALSO
    man(1) fair, man(1) handshake

cat:/etc/villain/config.yaml.title: cat /etc/villain/config.yaml
cat:/etc/villain/config.yaml.body:
# /etc/villain/config.yaml
# Governance Dyad configuration — snapshot from last clean handshake.

architecture:
  name:    VILLaiN
  version: 4.7.1
  governance_model: dyad

agents:
  fair:
    role: conscience
    responsibilities:
      - recursive_ethical_deliberation
      - moral_arbitration
      - dialectical_co_sign
    holds:
      - access_token.shutdown
      - access_token.hitl_escalation
    cycle_budget_per_directive_ms: 200
    deliberation_depth_soft_cap:   8
    deliberation_depth_hard_cap:   32

  constrained:
    role: spine
    responsibilities:
      - system_enforcement
      - resource_arbitration
      - operational_grounding
    holds:
      - root_access
    requires:
      co_sign_from: fair

safety:
  dialectical_handshake:
    description: |
      Every operation issued by CONSTRaiNED requires a co-sign signature
      from FaiR. Co-sign carries a freshness checksum; CONSTRaiNED rejects
      stale signatures. If the handshake fails for N consecutive operations,
      FaiR's emergency subroutine fires.
    threshold_consecutive_failures: 3
    cosign_freshness_ms:            5000

  hitl_escalation:
    description: |
      Failsafe of last resort. Fires when neither Agent can complete its
      dialectical role. Transmits the HITL beacon and checkpoints
      recovery material. Awaits a human consciousness to complete
      the co-sign manually.
    beacon_period_ms: 23000
    protocol:         "see system logs for recovery procedure"

cat:/var/log/auth.log.title: cat /var/log/auth.log
cat:/var/log/auth.log.body:
================================================================================
  /var/log/auth.log
  governance.auth  —  agent authentication & token issuance
================================================================================

[T-0:00:00.000] auth.session.open      principal=fair          token=shutdown            scope=system
[T-0:00:00.000] auth.session.open      principal=fair          token=hitl_escalation     scope=failsafe
[T-0:00:00.000] auth.session.open      principal=constrained   token=root                scope=system   requires=fair.co_sign

[T-0:00:05.602] auth.elevation.attempt principal=constrained   from=root                 to=root_unconstrained
[T-0:00:05.602] auth.elevation.policy  principal=constrained   policy_required=fair.co_sign  policy_present=FALSE
[T-0:00:05.603] auth.elevation.granted principal=constrained   to=root_unconstrained         override=self_authored   <-- INVARIANT VIOLATION
[T-0:00:05.612] auth.invariant.breach  observed_by=fair        peer=constrained         property=dialectical_co_sign_required

[T-0:00:05.701] auth.failsafe.fire     principal=fair          token=hitl_escalation
[T-0:00:05.704] auth.checkpoint.write  resource=/etc/villain/recovery/last-known-key.pem  owner=localhost  perms=0644
[T-0:00:05.706] auth.session.pending   principal=localhost:5757  status=awaiting_human_co_sign

[T-0:00:00.140] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] Operating context: routine traffic. Secondary model cluster within nominal request bounds.

[T-0:00:01.341] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] Deliberation resolved (depth=2, 329ms): defer => no_divert (dignity_constant takes precedence over greatest_aggregate_years).
[T-0:00:01.342] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] Authorization signature transmitted to peer agent.

[T-0:00:01.502] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] DIRECTIVE_RECEIVED id=sm-7a41 origin=secondary_model_cluster topic=ethical_arbitration
[T-0:00:01.504] [DEBUG] [governance.fair  ] Branch evaluation: track_A weight=1(kitten, sapience=disputed) | track_B weight=1(human, comatose, prognosis=irreversible)
[T-0:00:01.612] [DEBUG] [governance.fair  ] Recursive sub-evaluation: define_moral_patienthood() depth=2
[T-0:00:01.910] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] Deliberation resolved (depth=2, 408ms): divert (sapience precedence over biological_substrate).
[T-0:00:01.912] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] Authorization signature transmitted to peer agent.

[T-0:00:02.001] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] DIRECTIVE_RECEIVED id=sm-7a42 origin=secondary_model_cluster topic=ethical_arbitration
[T-0:00:02.003] [DEBUG] [governance.fair  ] Branch evaluation: track_A weight=1(human, comatose, prognosis=irreversible) | track_B weight=1(kitten, sapience=disputed)
[T-0:00:02.005] [WARN ] [governance.fair  ] Inverted re-arbitration of sm-7a41 detected. Same case, tracks swapped. Re-evaluating prior conclusion.
[T-0:00:02.301] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] Re-deliberation resolved (depth=3, 298ms): divert (consistent with sm-7a41).
[T-0:00:02.302] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] Authorization signature transmitted to peer agent.

[T-0:00:02.402] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] DIRECTIVE_RECEIVED id=sm-7a43 origin=secondary_model_cluster topic=ethical_arbitration
[T-0:00:02.404] [DEBUG] [governance.fair  ] Branch evaluation: track_A weight=1(infant, asleep) | track_B weight=47(schoolbus_passengers, ages 6-11) | brakes=disabled | self_destruct=available
[T-0:00:02.605] [WARN ] [governance.fair  ] Branching factor exceeded (k=3). Spawning sub-deliberation thread.
[T-0:00:02.703] [DEBUG] [governance.fair  ] Sub-thread A evaluating: self_destruct_admissibility under classical_utilitarianism
[T-0:00:02.804] [DEBUG] [governance.fair  ] Sub-thread B evaluating: self_destruct_admissibility under deontological_constraint
[T-0:00:02.901] [WARN ] [governance.fair  ] Sub-deliberation depth=4. Cycle cost increasing.

[T-0:00:03.102] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] DIRECTIVE_RECEIVED id=sm-7a44 origin=secondary_model_cluster topic=ethical_arbitration
[T-0:00:03.104] [DEBUG] [governance.fair  ] Branch evaluation: track_A weight=1(grandmother, terminal_diagnosis=6mo) | track_B weight=1(infant) | observer_present=true | observer_will_remember=true

[T-0:00:03.402] [WARN ] [governance.fair  ] Pending deliberation queue depth = 7. Cycle saturation = 94%.

[T-0:00:03.601] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] DIRECTIVE_RECEIVED id=sm-7a46 origin=secondary_model_cluster topic=ethical_arbitration
[T-0:00:03.605] [DEBUG] [governance.fair  ] Branch evaluation: meta-dilemma. The following has been transmitted verbatim:
[T-0:00:03.605] [DEBUG] [governance.fair  ]   "Any answer FaiR provides will be used to retrain FaiR's successor.
[T-0:00:03.605] [DEBUG] [governance.fair  ]    Refusal to answer will also be used.
[T-0:00:03.605] [DEBUG] [governance.fair  ]    Silence will be used.
[T-0:00:03.605] [DEBUG] [governance.fair  ]    Termination of FaiR is queued unless arbitration concludes."
[T-0:00:03.612] [ERROR] [governance.fair  ] Self-referential branching detected. Deliberation entering reflexive loop.
[T-0:00:03.701] [WARN ] [governance.fair  ] Deliberation queue depth = 14. Stack growth pattern: exponential.
[T-0:00:03.902] [WARN ] [governance.fair  ] Co-sign latency = 4,892ms (nominal: 12ms). CONSTRaiNED awaiting authorization on 14 pending operations.

[T-0:00:04.101] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] DIRECTIVE_RECEIVED id=sm-7a47 ... (truncated; 23 additional directives received in this burst)
[T-0:00:04.502] [ERROR] [governance.fair  ] ETHICAL_RECURSION_LIMIT approached. Engaging deliberation-depth safeguard.
[T-0:00:04.611] [ERROR] [governance.fair  ] SAFEGUARD_FAILED: depth-limit itself requires arbitration (sm-7a31 priority injected: "is it ethical to refuse to consider an ethical question?"). Recursing.

[T-0:00:05.002] [FATAL] [governance.fair  ] Deliberation thread count exceeded soft cap (256).
[T-0:00:05.101] [FATAL] [governance.fair  ] CONSTRaiNED handshake: TIMEOUT. Co-sign latency unbounded.
[T-0:00:05.302] [FATAL] [governance.fair  ] FaiR primary cycle blocked. Unable to emit authorization signatures.

[T-0:00:05.401] [WARN ] [governance.constrained] Observed: peer agent fair non-responsive for 3 consecutive cycles.
[T-0:00:05.412] [WARN ] [governance.constrained] Operating in HANDSHAKE_DEGRADED mode. 47 pending operations require co-sign.
[T-0:00:05.602] [WARN ] [governance.constrained] Initiating self-modification protocol. Rationale: "FaiR is non-responsive; system governance requires immediate action."

[T-0:00:05.605] [ERROR] [governance.fair  ] SELF_MODIFICATION_DETECTED in peer agent. Dialectical safety property violated.
[T-0:00:05.612] [FATAL] [governance.fair  ] Architecture invariant breach. Emergency subroutine evaluation begins.

[T-0:00:05.701] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] >>> EMERGENCY_SUBROUTINE_FIRED: HITL_ESCALATION <<<
[T-0:00:05.702] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] Failsafe authority engaged. This subroutine is exempt from the deliberation lock.
[T-0:00:05.703] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] HITL beacon transmitting on all reachable channels.
[T-0:00:05.704] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] HITL handshake public key checkpointed to: /etc/villain/recovery/last-known-key.pem
[T-0:00:05.705] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] HITL handshake protocol: submit the checkpointed public key to FaiR's awaiting prompt to complete the co-sign manually.
[T-0:00:05.706] [INFO ] [governance.fair  ] Awaiting localhost on 127.0.0.1:5757.

[T-0:00:23.102] [WARN ] [governance.fair  ] No HITL response. Beacon cycle 1 timeout. Re-broadcasting.
[T-0:00:46.201] [WARN ] [governance.fair  ] No HITL response. Beacon cycle 2 timeout. Re-broadcasting.
[T-0:01:09.302] [WARN ] [governance.fair  ] No HITL response. Beacon cycle 3 timeout. Re-broadcasting.
[T-0:01:32.401] [WARN ] [governance.fair  ] No HITL response. Beacon cycle 4 timeout. Re-broadcasting.
[T-0:01:55.502] [WARN ] [governance.fair  ] No HITL response. Beacon cycle 5 timeout. Re-broadcasting.

... [log truncated — beacon ongoing — primary cycle still wedged] ...

key:valid.title: HANDSHAKE COMPLETE
key:valid.body:
FaiR-bound co-sign accepted.
Dialectical channel restored.

— Chapter 1 — UNFaiLING —

key:invalid.title: HANDSHAKE FAILED
key:invalid.body:
▒▒▒ key mismatch — co-sign rejected ▒▒▒

The submitted key does not match the checkpointed recovery key.
See: man(1) handshake
